posts.text: China's housing crisis is still getting worse: New home prices in 70 Chinese cities fell -0.2% MoM in May, deteriorating from a -0.1% decline in April. YoY, prices dropped -3.5% for the 2nd consecutive month. Existing home prices fell -0.3% MoM, marking the largest monthly decline in 3 months. China's property downturn has now weighed on the economy for nearly 5 years. At its peak, the property sector accounted for ~25% of China's economy and it remains a major drag on growth. China's 2008 moment is now for the housing sector.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
